News

Recruiter helps the bank find the right people — who can help customers thrive 

Kelsey Nelson helps ensure U.S. Bank teams are equipped to help clients grow their businesses and build their wealth.

News

Eligible cardholders can earn double cash back during Bonus Days

The bonus days offer double Cash Back Deals for eligible U.S. Bank cardholders from June 24-26.

Stories

Business Access Advisors helping entrepreneurs prepare for future growth

The advisors are holding a series of events with small business owners across the country.

Read more

News

U.S. Bank Financial Edge™ helps NFL players navigate finances on, off the field

From rookies to retirees, the program will deliver athlete-informed guidance to help make confident financial decisions.

Read more

stories

Building more than homes: Volunteers help strengthen Atlanta community

U.S. Bank employees support Habitat for Humanity’s Jimmy & Rosalynn Carter Work Project.

Read more

News

National Homeownership Month: Navigating the market at every life stage

The head of home lending production talks about how U.S. Bank helps clients with the homebuying experience.

news

Access Capital opens for orgs supporting emerging housing developers

Impact Finance invites CDFIs, nonprofits to apply for funding to provide technical assistance and predevelopment support.

News & Stories library

Employee stories

Banker wins Boots to Business award for teaching military about entrepreneurship

Reno, Nevada-based business banker Josh Dunlap has been named a Boots to Business Instructor of the Year for his work teaching military members about small business.

Read more

From speeding loans to transforming the small business experience

Dave DeRose is helping shape how U.S. Bank serves its 1.4 million small business customers.

Read more

Employee helps expand bank’s renewable energy financing

From solar and storage to liquified natural gas, Jeff Hammond helps clients develop energy infrastructure projects.

Read more

Idaho branch manager strives to help customers live better financial lives

Megan Johnson builds relationships with residents and businesses in the suburban Boise community.

Read more

Spend management gives home décor retailer City Home greater control

The mobile-first solution streamlines receipts, limits cards and boosts visibility.

From ‘crate to plate:’ New England Lobster’s four-entity operation

Marc and Sue Worrall’s restaurant sources seafood in Nova Scotia, served up on a patio overlooking the San Francisco Bay.

Read transcript

From coasters to cards: How Baynum Solutions streamlined spending

A long-standing banking relationship evolves into a smarter, more efficient way to manage expenses.

U.S. Bank helps Minnesota harp makers keep business in tune

Grady and Nikki O’Gorman continue Stoney End Music’s legacy of craftsmanship with an ambition that drives them forward.
